content.problemStatement: Lately, Chef has been inspired by the "pawri" meme. Therefore, he decided to take a string SSS and change each of its substrings that spells "party" to "pawri". Find the resulting string.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string S consisting of only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the string after every occurrence of the substring "party" has been replaced by "pawri".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: No occurrence of "party", string stays unchanged.\nTest case 2: "party" appears twice (positions 1‑5 and 12‑16) and is replaced by "pawri" each time.\nTest case 3: Exactly one occurrence of "party" is replaced.

solution.approach: Scan the string from left to right. Whenever the next five characters equal "party", append "pawri" to the answer and skip five positions; otherwise, copy the current character and move one step forward. Because the replacement has the same length as the original pattern, overlapping occurrences cannot arise, and a single linear pass suffices. This yields O(|S|) time per test case and O(|S|) auxiliary space for the output string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) — each character is examined at most once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S|) for the output string (in‑place modification is also possible).
